fields.Name: MRAA Skip 01 - Membership Composition
fields.Description: Conversation 1: Break down 15K member accounts into paying tiers vs staff vs non-members

InputDefinition.userMessage: I'm presenting to the board next week and I've been saying we have 15,000 members. But I know that includes staff accounts and non-members. Help me build an honest picture — break down our accounts into paying dealer memberships at each tier versus staff accounts, non-members, and partners. I need to know our real paying member count versus our total platform reach.

judgeValidationCriteria: Response distinguishes between paying membership tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold) and non-paying account types (staff, non-member)
Query uses YourMembership.Member table grouped by MemberTypeCode
Visualization shows the composition breakdown clearly
Response references the Membership table for tier pricing context

criteria: Response distinguishes paying tiers from staff/non-member accounts
Includes counts per membership type
Provides a meaningful visualization of the composition

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 02 - Non-Member Conversion Targets
fields.Description: Conversation 1: Identify top organizations with unconverted Retail Non-Members

InputDefinition.userMessage: Those 5,000+ Retail Non-Members — they're already on our platform but not paying. Analyze which organizations they come from and cross-reference with whether those orgs already have paying members. I want to see the conversion opportunity by organization size — who has the most untapped accounts?

judgeValidationCriteria: Query filters for MemberTypeCode containing 'Non_Member' or similar
Results grouped by Organization showing non-member count
Cross-references with paying members in same organizations
Ranks organizations by conversion opportunity

criteria: Identifies organizations with most non-member accounts
Shows whether those orgs also have paying members
Provides actionable conversion opportunity ranking

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 03 - Expiration Data Quality
fields.Description: Conversation 1: Segment members by expiration status — real vs bogus legacy dates

InputDefinition.userMessage: Now help me understand retention. I see members with expiration dates all over the map — some from 2012, some next month. Segment the membership by expiration status: genuinely expiring soon, already lapsed but still Active, and those with clearly bogus legacy dates from the YourMembership migration. I need to know the data quality situation before I can trust any retention metrics.

judgeValidationCriteria: Segments members into meaningful expiration buckets
Identifies bogus dates (e.g., before 2020 or year 0001)
Separates genuinely lapsed from data quality issues
Provides counts for each segment

criteria: Correctly identifies bogus legacy expiration dates
Separates real expirations from data migration artifacts
Provides actionable data quality assessment

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 04 - Renewal Priority List
fields.Description: Conversation 1: Members expiring in 90 days with revenue at risk by org

InputDefinition.userMessage: For the members genuinely expiring in the next 90 days, build me a renewal priority list. Show each member's organization, tier, state, and annual dues at risk. But also flag which organizations would lose ALL their paying members if these don't renew — those are the accounts where we'd lose the relationship entirely.

judgeValidationCriteria: Filters members with valid expiration dates in next 90 days
Joins to Membership table for dues amounts
Identifies organizations at risk of losing all paying members
Sorts by revenue at risk

criteria: Shows members expiring within 90 days with dues at risk
Joins Member to Membership for pricing
Flags organizations that would lose all paying members

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 05 - Geographic vs Association Alignment
fields.Description: Conversation 1: Compare dealer concentration by state against Marine Trade Association groups

InputDefinition.userMessage: One more for the board — show me where our paying dealers are concentrated versus where Marine Trade Associations exist in our group network. Are there states where we have a strong association relationship but few paying members, or states packed with dealers but no association partner? That alignment gap is where our growth opportunity sits.

judgeValidationCriteria: Query uses Member table for geographic distribution by State
Query uses Group/GroupType tables for Marine Trade Associations
Cross-references geographic concentration with association presence
Identifies alignment gaps between member density and association coverage

criteria: Queries both Member and Group/GroupType tables
Shows state-level comparison of members vs associations
Identifies growth opportunity gaps

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 06 - Complete Revenue Breakdown
fields.Description: Conversation 2: Gross vs net revenue across all streams with GL codes

InputDefinition.userMessage: Show me every revenue stream flowing through invoices: dues, store orders, donations, certifications. But I need the full picture — gross revenue AND net after discounts, credits, and refunds. Break it out by GL code so I can see which categories actually drive income versus which ones are mostly offsets.

judgeValidationCriteria: Query uses InvoiceItem table with breakdowns by InvoiceType and LineItemType
Shows gross and net amounts accounting for discounts/credits/refunds
References GLCode data for category-level analysis
Includes donation fund context from DonationFund table

criteria: Shows gross and net revenue by stream
Accounts for discounts, credits, and refunds
Uses GL code breakdown for category analysis

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 07 - Dues Revenue Composition Trend
fields.Description: Conversation 2: Annual dues trend 2015-present broken down by membership type

InputDefinition.userMessage: Zoom into the dues trend from 2015 to today. We peaked at $1.3M in 2022. I don't just want the line — break it down by the membership types being purchased each year. Did the peak come from more members joining, members upgrading to higher tiers, or price increases? Show me the composition of revenue, not just the total.

judgeValidationCriteria: Query uses DuesTransaction with year grouping and membership type breakdown
Shows revenue trend from 2015 onward
Breaks down by MembershipRequested or similar field
References Membership table for tier pricing context

criteria: Shows yearly dues revenue trend
Breaks down by membership type purchased
Provides composition analysis not just totals

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 08 - Revenue Diversification Analysis
fields.Description: Conversation 2: Non-dues revenue trends by product category and GL code

InputDefinition.userMessage: The board keeps asking about revenue diversification. Show me how non-dues revenue streams have performed over time — store sales by product category, certification fees, conference tickets, donations by fund. Which categories are growing and which are flat? I want to see if we're actually diversifying or just talking about it.

judgeValidationCriteria: Separates non-dues revenue from dues revenue
Uses InvoiceItem, GLCode, ProductCategory, and DonationFund tables
Shows trends over time for each non-dues category
Identifies growing vs flat revenue streams

criteria: Analyzes non-dues revenue separately
Uses multiple tables for categorization
Shows trends and identifies growth patterns

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 09 - Revenue Concentration Risk
fields.Description: Conversation 2: Top organizations by total spend with concentration curve

InputDefinition.userMessage: Revenue concentration — if our top 5 member organizations stopped paying tomorrow, what's the impact? Show me the top 20 organizations by total lifetime spend across ALL invoice types, and calculate each one's share of total revenue. I need to see the Pareto curve and know exactly how exposed we are.

judgeValidationCriteria: Aggregates total spend by organization across all invoice types
Calculates percentage share of total revenue
Shows top 20 organizations ranked by spend
Presents concentration risk clearly

criteria: Shows top organizations by total spend
Calculates revenue share percentages
Communicates concentration risk clearly

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 10 - Pricing Integrity Check
fields.Description: Conversation 2: Compare published pricing across Membership, HubSpot Products, and actual collections

InputDefinition.userMessage: Pricing integrity check. Compare our published membership pricing in the Membership table and HubSpot product catalog against what we're actually collecting in dues transactions. Factor in promo code discounts and credits from our invoices. What's our effective price per tier after all adjustments? Are we holding our pricing or quietly eroding it?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Membership table for published prices
Queries HubSpot Product table for CRM pricing
Calculates effective price from DuesTransaction and InvoiceItem adjustments
Compares all three pricing sources

criteria: Compares pricing across multiple sources
Calculates effective price after adjustments
Cross-system query spanning YourMembership and HubSpot

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 11 - Program Portfolio Overview
fields.Description: Conversation 3: Full view of certifications, product categories, tickets, donation funds

InputDefinition.userMessage: Show me MRAA's full program portfolio in one view — every certification with CEU requirements, all product categories we sell through the store, event ticket types with pricing, and donation funds. I want to see the breadth of what we offer and what each program costs a member. Are there pricing gaps or overlaps?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Certification, ProductCategory, EventTicket, and DonationFund tables
Shows pricing for each program type
Presents a unified portfolio view
Identifies pricing relationships between programs

criteria: Queries all four program-related tables
Shows pricing and requirements for each
Provides unified portfolio view

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 12 - Events Calendar with Traction
fields.Description: Conversation 3: Events with registration counts and registration form status

InputDefinition.userMessage: Now show me the events calendar — conferences, certification cohorts, everything. For each event, show dates, virtual versus in-person, whether a registration form is set up, and how many people registered. Help me spot the underperformers — which programs have zero traction despite having infrastructure in place?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Event, EventRegistration, and EventRegistrationForm tables
Shows registration count per event
Identifies events with forms but zero registrations
Distinguishes virtual from in-person events

criteria: Joins Event with EventRegistration and EventRegistrationForm
Shows registration counts and form availability
Identifies underperforming events

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 13 - Summit Attendee Analysis
fields.Description: Conversation 3: Industry Summit registrants cross-referenced with membership tiers

InputDefinition.userMessage: For the upcoming MRAA Industry Summit — who's in the room? Show every registrant with their organization and registration date. Cross-reference with our membership data — what tier are they? Are we getting Gold dealers to the Summit or mostly non-members getting a free look? The composition tells me how we're positioning the event.

judgeValidationCriteria: Filters EventRegistration for the Industry Summit event
Cross-references registrants with Member table for tier info
Shows membership tier distribution of attendees
Identifies non-member vs paying member composition

criteria: Identifies Industry Summit registrants
Cross-references with membership data
Analyzes tier composition of attendees

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 14 - HubSpot Product Catalog vs Deal Line Items
fields.Description: Conversation 3: Compare product list prices against actual deal line item amounts

InputDefinition.userMessage: Flip to what we're selling through HubSpot — partner memberships, booth packages, certifications, dealer scorecards. Show the product catalog alongside actual deal line items. Are we selling at list price or discounting? What products move fastest? Compare these prices to what we charge through YourMembership — I want to see if our two sales channels are aligned or contradicting each other.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries HubSpot Product, LineItem, and Deal tables
Compares list prices to actual line item amounts
Cross-references with YourMembership pricing (EventTicket or Membership)
Identifies discounting patterns and channel alignment

criteria: Queries HubSpot Product and LineItem tables
Compares list vs actual prices
Cross-system pricing comparison

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 15 - Cross-Channel Pricing Drift
fields.Description: Conversation 3: Three-source pricing comparison for overlapping products

InputDefinition.userMessage: Connect the dots across all three pricing sources. Our certification tickets are $2,250 in YourMembership, our HubSpot products show different numbers, and our actual invoices show what we collected. Show me the pricing for every product that exists in more than one system — certifications, memberships, conferences — side by side. Where is there drift between what we publish, what we quote, and what we collect?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries EventTicket, HubSpot Product, InvoiceItem, Membership, and DuesTransaction
Identifies products that exist in multiple systems
Shows pricing side-by-side from each source
Identifies drift or inconsistencies

criteria: Cross-system query spanning 3+ pricing sources
Identifies pricing inconsistencies
Compares published, quoted, and collected amounts

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 16 - Deal Pipeline by Product Type
fields.Description: Conversation 4: Pipeline funnel categorized by what's being sold

InputDefinition.userMessage: Show me the HubSpot deal pipeline as a funnel — count and total dollar value at each stage. But categorize the deals by what they're selling: booth space, partner memberships, education sponsorships, scorecards. I want to see where the money sits by product type AND stage — are the big deals at the top of the funnel or near close?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Deal table grouped by dealstage
Categorizes deals by product type from dealname or LineItem
Shows count and total value per stage
Presents as funnel visualization

criteria: Shows deal pipeline by stage with dollar values
Categorizes deals by product type
Funnel or stage-based visualization

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 17 - Sales Activity vs Deal Creation
fields.Description: Conversation 4: Activity trends (calls, meetings, tasks) correlated with new deals

InputDefinition.userMessage: Now the team's activity patterns. Show outbound versus inbound calls per month, meetings scheduled versus completed, tasks open versus done — all trending over time. Layer deal creation dates on top. I want to see if activity spikes correlate with new deals entering the pipeline, or if the team is busy but not productive.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Call, Meeting, Task, and Deal tables
Shows monthly trends for each activity type
Distinguishes call direction (inbound vs outbound)
Overlays deal creation timeline for correlation analysis

criteria: Shows activity trends across multiple HubSpot tables
Correlates activity with deal creation
Distinguishes productive vs unproductive activity

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 18 - Sales Team Leaderboard
fields.Description: Conversation 4: Owner-level breakdown of calls, notes, meetings, tasks, and deals

InputDefinition.userMessage: Build me a team leaderboard. Calls logged, notes written, meetings held, tasks completed — by HubSpot owner. Show deals associated with each owner too. I want to see who's doing activity AND closing versus who's just logging touches. Pull the most recent notes from each person so I can gauge the quality of intelligence they're capturing.

judgeValidationCriteria: Aggregates Call, Note, Meeting, Task by hubspot_owner_id
Includes Deal count/value per owner
Shows sample notes for quality assessment
Ranks owners by combined activity and results

criteria: Aggregates multiple activity types by owner
Includes deal performance alongside activity
Provides note samples for quality context

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 19 - Hot Prospects and Pipeline Leakage
fields.Description: Conversation 4: Customer/opportunity contacts with engagement and meetings without deals

InputDefinition.userMessage: Show me contacts in 'customer' and 'opportunity' lifecycle stages with their companies. When were they last contacted, what's the latest note, and do they have open deals? Flag anyone not touched in 30+ days — those are hot prospects we might be neglecting. Are there companies with meetings but no deal created? That's pipeline leakage.

judgeValidationCriteria: Filters Contact by lifecyclestage for customer/opportunity
Joins to Company for org context
References Note for latest intelligence
Identifies contacts/companies with meetings but no deal

criteria: Identifies hot prospects by lifecycle stage
Flags neglected contacts
Identifies pipeline leakage from meetings without deals

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 20 - Sales Intelligence from Notes
fields.Description: Conversation 4: Analyze HubSpot note content for recurring themes and market signals

InputDefinition.userMessage: Analyze the content of our recent HubSpot notes — meetings at ICAST, Dealer Week booth interest, partnership discussions. What topics keep coming up? What are prospects asking about? I want to know what the market is telling our sales team that might not be reaching leadership. Surface the patterns in what people are actually talking about.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries HubSpot Note table for recent entries
Analyzes note body content (hs_note_body or hs_body_preview)
Identifies recurring themes or topics
Surfaces market intelligence from sales conversations

criteria: Analyzes note content for themes
Surfaces actionable market intelligence
Goes beyond simple aggregation to content analysis

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 21 - CRM Coverage by Membership Tier
fields.Description: Conversation 5: Match members to HubSpot contacts by email, show coverage gaps by tier

InputDefinition.userMessage: Match our 15,000 MRAA members to HubSpot contacts by email. Show the coverage gap by membership tier — what percentage of Gold, Silver, and Bronze members are tracked in CRM versus invisible to the sales team? Then show how coverage varies by lifecycle stage — are our CRM-tracked members mostly 'leads' or actual 'customers'?

judgeValidationCriteria: Joins YourMembership.Member to HubSpot.Contact on email
Groups by MemberTypeCode showing match vs no-match counts
Calculates coverage percentage per tier
Shows lifecycle stage distribution for matched contacts

criteria: Cross-system JOIN between YourMembership and HubSpot on email
Shows CRM coverage gap by membership tier
Includes lifecycle stage analysis for matched contacts

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 22 - High-Value Members Missing from CRM
fields.Description: Conversation 5: Gold/Silver members with no HubSpot contact, check company-level presence

InputDefinition.userMessage: For every Gold and Silver member with NO HubSpot contact — list them with organization, state, and expiration date. Then check if their organization appears as a HubSpot company even though the individual is missing. Are we engaging the company but missing the key decision-makers? That's a different problem than having no relationship at all.

judgeValidationCriteria: LEFT JOIN Member to Contact filtering for NULL matches
Filters for Gold and Silver tier members
Cross-references Organization with HubSpot Company names
Distinguishes company-engaged vs completely unknown

criteria: Identifies high-value members missing from CRM
Three-way cross-system query: Member, Contact, Company
Distinguishes missing-individual vs missing-relationship

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 23 - Expiring Members Segmented by CRM Status
fields.Description: Conversation 5: Members expiring in 90 days split by CRM engagement level

InputDefinition.userMessage: The retention play. Cross-reference members expiring in the next 90 days against HubSpot. Split them into three groups: those with an active deal (great — sales is engaged), those with a contact but no deal (known but not acted on), and those with zero CRM presence (completely off radar). Each group needs a different intervention strategy.

judgeValidationCriteria: Filters Member for valid expirations in next 90 days
LEFT JOINs to Contact and Deal on email
Segments into three distinct groups based on CRM engagement
Provides counts and member details for each segment

criteria: Segments expiring members by CRM engagement level
Three-tier segmentation: deal, contact-only, invisible
Cross-system query joining Member, Contact, Deal

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 24 - Non-Member Warm Handoff Targets
fields.Description: Conversation 5: Retail Non-Members at orgs with active HubSpot deals

InputDefinition.userMessage: Flip the lens — show me Retail Non-Members whose organizations already have active HubSpot deals. These are people at companies where our sales team is already in conversation about partnerships or booth space, but the individuals haven't converted to paying members. That's a warm handoff the team should be making today.

judgeValidationCriteria: Filters Member for Retail Non-Member type
Cross-references Organization with Deal company names
Identifies individuals at companies with active deals
Presents actionable conversion opportunity list

criteria: Identifies non-members at organizations with active deals
Cross-system query joining Member data to HubSpot Deal data
Presents actionable warm handoff list

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 25 - Key Accounts Cross-System Dashboard
fields.Description: Conversation 5: Organizations in both systems ranked by combined relationship depth

InputDefinition.userMessage: Build me the ultimate key accounts view. Find organizations in BOTH systems. For each, show: total MRAA members by tier, total dues collected, HubSpot deal pipeline value, call and meeting count, and the latest note. Rank by combined relationship depth. These are our top partnerships across both platforms — I want to see the full picture in one dashboard I can check weekly.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Member, DuesTransaction, Company, Deal, Call, Meeting, and Note tables
Matches organizations across both systems
Aggregates multiple metrics per organization
Ranks by combined relationship value

criteria: Multi-table cross-system aggregation spanning 7+ tables
Shows combined relationship metrics per org
Ranks organizations by combined value

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 26 - Association Network vs Member Density
fields.Description: Conversation 6: Marine Trade Association coverage gaps against member geography

InputDefinition.userMessage: Show me our Marine Trade Association network alongside our member geographic footprint. Which state associations have lots of MRAA members in their territory, which have few, and which states have members but no association partner at all? The gaps between our association relationships and our member density are where we should be building new partnerships.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Group and GroupType for Marine Trade Associations
Queries Member for state-level distribution
Joins Location data for geographic context
Identifies alignment gaps between associations and members

criteria: Cross-references Group network with member geography
Identifies states with gaps in association coverage
Uses Group, GroupType, Member, and Location tables

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 27 - Sponsor vs Partner vs Deal Alignment
fields.Description: Conversation 6: Compare sponsor rotators against partner memberships and HubSpot deals

InputDefinition.userMessage: We feature sponsors on our platform. Show me who they are alongside our top partner members and HubSpot deal pipeline. Are our sponsors also our deepest paying relationships? Or are there partner members with significant dues who we haven't tapped for sponsorship? Show me the whitespace between sponsorship, membership, and deal revenue.

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries SponsorRotator for current sponsors
Queries Member for partner-tier memberships
Queries Deal for pipeline value
Identifies untapped sponsorship opportunities

criteria: Queries SponsorRotator alongside membership and deal data
Identifies alignment between sponsors and paying partners
Surfaces whitespace opportunities

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 28 - Payment Infrastructure Analysis
fields.Description: Conversation 6: Payment processor utilization and member payment behavior patterns

InputDefinition.userMessage: Help me understand our payment infrastructure. Different processors handle dues, store orders, donations, and foundation contributions. Show me the volume and revenue flowing through each processor and payment method. Do high-tier members pay differently than Bronze members? Is there friction in our payment setup that might be costing us conversions?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries PaymentProcessor table for infrastructure overview
Analyzes InvoiceItem and DuesTransaction for payment method distribution
Cross-references payment behavior with Member tier
Identifies potential friction points

criteria: Analyzes payment processor utilization
Shows payment method distribution by tier
Identifies infrastructure patterns and friction

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 29 - International Membership Analysis
fields.Description: Conversation 6: International member footprint with country breakdown and Canadian comparison

InputDefinition.userMessage: I want to understand our international footprint. We have members in 21 countries — show me the breakdown with member counts, tiers, and revenue per country. Cross-reference with our location and timezone data. For our Canadian members specifically — they're our biggest international segment — how do their membership tiers and dues compare to US members? Are we pricing appropriately for international markets?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Member grouped by Country
References Country, Location, and TimeZone tables
Compares Canadian vs US member tier distribution and revenue
Analyzes international pricing appropriateness

criteria: Shows international member distribution across countries
Uses Country, Location, and TimeZone tables
Compares Canadian vs US membership composition

fields.Name: MRAA Skip 30 - West Marine 360 View
fields.Description: Conversation 6: Complete cross-system 360 view of largest member organization

InputDefinition.userMessage: Build me a complete 360-degree view of West Marine — our largest member organization with nearly 200 accounts. Pull everything across both systems: all members by type, complete dues and invoice history, event registrations, matching HubSpot contacts with lifecycle stages, deals in pipeline, call logs, meeting history, notes, and open tasks. Then tell me — if I ran this template for every top-20 organization, what patterns should I be looking for?

judgeValidationCriteria: Queries Member, DuesTransaction, InvoiceItem, EventRegistration for YourMembership data
Queries Contact, Company, Deal, Call, Meeting, Note, Task for HubSpot data
Filters all tables for West Marine organization
Provides comprehensive multi-tab or multi-section view spanning 10+ tables

criteria: Comprehensive cross-system query spanning 10+ tables
All data filtered for West Marine organization
Provides actionable 360-degree relationship view
